output.summary: Added headingSize attribute to ContentWithMedia block. Attribute allows editors to choose between Regular (H2) and Small (H3) heading sizes via a ToggleGroupControl in the Settings panel. Implementation follows the exact pattern from FeaturedPost block. All three files (block.json, TSX, PHP) updated and kept in sync. Test page updated with a 15th scenario testing headingSize=small.

attributesAdded.name: headingSize
attributesAdded.description: Controls the heading level rendered by ThemeHeading. 'regular' maps to H2 (headingSize=2), 'small' maps to H3 (headingSize=3).

blockJson.change: Added headingSize attribute with type string, default 'regular', enum ['small', 'regular']
blockJson.location: After heading attribute, before description attribute

changes: Added ToggleGroupControl in Settings PanelBody with Regular (H2) and Small (H3) options, following FeaturedPost pattern exactly
Changed ThemeHeading headingSize prop from hardcoded '2' to 'attributes.headingSize === "small" ? 3 : 2'
Changed ThemeHeading 'headingSize' parameter from hardcoded '2' to '$headingLevel'

output.patternFollowed: FeaturedPost block (blocks/FeaturedPost/) — exact same headingSize attribute definition in block.json, same ToggleGroupControl UI, same mapping logic

buildStatus.notes: Build compiles with only pre-existing performance warnings (asset size limits)

lintStatus.notes: All 4 warnings are pre-existing (experimental WP API usage, React Hook deps). No new errors introduced.

syncStatus.notes: TSX and PHP kept in perfect sync. Both map 'small' to heading level 3 and 'regular' to heading level 2. Both pass the computed level to ThemeHeading.

addedScenario.title: Heading Size - Small (H3)
addedScenario.description: headingSize=small renders an H3 heading instead of the default H2. Tests the headingSize attribute mapping through ThemeHeading.
